steps.title: Fastest path: mark the circle facts first
steps.content: Start with the circle facts: radii are equal, a diameter sets up a right angle, a tangent is perpendicular to the radius, and central/inscribed angles connect to arcs.

steps.title: Apply the circumference formula
steps.content: The circumference of a circle is $C=\pi d$, where $d$ is the diameter. Substitute the given $C=18\pi$: $$18\pi=\pi d.$$

steps.title: Solve for the diameter
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\pi$: $$d=18.$$ The answer is 18.
